channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La capital mundial de las papas fritas tiene un problema: demasiadas papas
item.description: Bélgica tuvo una cosecha récord justo cuando los aranceles del gobierno de Donald Trump redujeron la demanda estadounidense de papas fritas congeladas y la competencia asiática se intensificó.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross

media:content.media:credit: Kevin Faingnaert for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Targets Brazil With 25% Tariff, Citing Unfair Trade Practices
item.description: The proposal marks the latest effort by the administration to rebuild its tariff agenda through Section 301 investigations.
item.dc:creator: Daisuke Wakabayashi

media:content.media:credit: Rodolfo Buhrer/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Bulk carriers at the port of Paranagua, Brazil, in March.

item.title: Trump podría ganar un importante aliado en las próximas elecciones de Colombia
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, un candidato de línea dura de la derecha que se enfrenta a un rival de izquierda en una segunda vuelta presidencial, ha prometido aplastar a los narcotraficantes.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal and Luis Ferré-Sadurní
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Abelardo de la Espriella, candidato de extrema derecha a la presidencia de Colombia, cerró su campaña en la ciudad de Medellín.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Appears to Pull Back From Threat to Strike Beirut
item.description: President Trump is pressing Israel not to attack the Lebanese capital. An escalation in Israel’s campaign against Hezbollah could threaten peace talks with Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein guardó su esperma en un banco. Podría seguir almacenado
item.description: Epstein dejó su esperma en un banco varios años antes de su muerte y dijo que si moría, debía quedar bajo el control de su patrimonio.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Mroz and Maggie Astor

media:content.media:credit: Claire Merchlinsky/The New York Times; Fotografías de Getty

item.title: A Congressman Was Suddenly Absent. So We Looked. And Looked.
item.description: Will the mystery of Tom Kean Jr., a New Jersey representative, finally be solved on Tuesday? The reporters covering the story will have questions.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Seng for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Tom Kean has not been seen for nearly three months but is on the ballot in Tuesday’s New Jersey primary.

item.title: Mamdani Advances 34th St. Bus Lane Plan That U.S. Had Blocked
item.description: The city is moving forward with a redesign of 34th Street in Manhattan that the White House halted last year after threatening to withhold federal funding for other transit priorities.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Buses on 34th Street in Midtown Manhattan move at about five miles per hour.

item.title: Group Pledges $2 Million to Fight AIPAC in House Races
item.description: A super PAC created to counter spending from powerful pro-Israel groups will try to help Democrats critical of Israel’s wars and military actions.
item.dc:creator: Maya King and Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times; Bing Guan for The New York Times; Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: American Priorities intends to spend $2 million to help three House candidates in New York. From left to right: Claire Valdez, Brad Lander and Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 2, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How Three Chess Friends Battled Demons and Saved Two Lives
item.description: A $486 studio apartment on Central Park kept the friends connected, and it almost tore them apart.
item.dc:creator: John Leland and Juan Arredondo

media:content.media:description: Chess was what drew the friends together — the same rules and chances for everyone who played.

item.title: Trump Claims a Missing N.J. Congressman Has Been ‘Working Tirelessly’
item.description: The message from President Trump about Representative Thomas Kean Jr. comes as voters head to the polls on Tuesday. Mr. Kean’s name is on the ballot, but he hasn’t been seen for months.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call, via Associated Press Images
media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr., a Republican, has not been seen in Congress or on the campaign trail in nearly three months.

item.title: Mamdani Is Under Pressure to Act Amid Slowing Job Growth in New York
item.description: As New York City faces slowing job growth and broader economic concerns, Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not offered a detailed plan to create jobs.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York City has added jobs this year, though it lost 3,100 private-sector jobs in March, the most recent month for which data is available.

item.title: Russia Launches Deadly Strikes on Ukraine
item.description: Missiles and drones struck the cities of Kyiv and Dnipro, Ukrainian officials said. At least 10 people were killed.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova

media:content.media:credit: Eugene Kotenko/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An explosion in Kyiv, Ukraine, during a Russian strike on Tuesday morning.

item.title: Late Night Updates America on the Lineup for Its 250th Birthday Bash
item.description: “Instead of music, the entertainment will be an 80-year-old man yelling about windmills,” Jimmy Kimmel said after President Trump said he would headline a summer concert series.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: El movimiento de Alberta para separarse de Canadá llega a las urnas
item.description: En octubre, los habitantes de Alberta podrán decidir si quieren permanecer en Canadá o celebrar un referendo para separarse. ¿Resolverá el asunto o profundizará la división?
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff and Amber Bracken

media:content.media:description: Una protesta contra Danielle Smith y el Partido Conservador Unido en Edmonton el viernes

item.title: Word of the Day: parsimonious
item.description: This word has appeared in three articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Putin Has No Good Way Out of His War
item.description: Perpetuating a war is not the same as winning one.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kimmage

item.title: ¿Se pueden usar calcetines con sandalias?
item.description: Una profesora se pregunta sobre las normas de etiqueta del calzado contemporáneo.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fashion or faux pas? On the street at Paris Fashion Week.

item.title: Quote of the Day:Trump’s Sway in Primaries May Not Translate to the Midterms
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, June 2, 2024.

item.title: The New Zealand Parakeet Pair That Are Saving Their Species
item.description: There are about 450 orange-fronted parakeets left in the world. Super breeders Nacho and Trixie are doing their best to bolster the population.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:credit: Leigh Percasky/The Isaac Conservation and Wildlife Trust, via AFP — Getty Images

item.title: Corrections: June 2, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, June 2, 2026.

item.title: Security Council Members Call for Israel to Withdraw From Southern Lebanon
item.description: The U.N. panel met after Israel had threatened to attack southern Beirut. President Trump said later that Israel and Hezbollah had agreed not to attack each other.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fleeing the southern suburbs of Beirut, a family sheltered on the roadside Monday amid growing traffic, after Israel warned the area would be targeted.

item.title: Hawaii Man Charged With Killing Three Men
item.description: An expansive manhunt on the Big Island of Hawaii ended with the suspect’s arrest on Thursday in the deaths of the men, who were in their 60s and 70s.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Deborah Davis, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The scene of the arrest last week in Kaimu, Hawaii. A large manhunt led the authorities to the suspect.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 2, 2026
item.description: Rich Katz is here to cheer you up.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Joel Reichenberger for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Don’t get “piste” about today’s puzzle. We’re here to help.

item.title: Trump Administration Announces Stricter Rules for Medicaid Work Requirement
item.description: By tightening an exception for people with serious illnesses, more of them may lose coverage.
item.dc:creator: Margot Sanger-Katz and Sarah Kliff

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mehmet Oz, President Trump and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. are reshaping Medicaid rules.

item.title: As Ebola Spreads, Scientists Race to Find Vaccines and Treatments
item.description: The Bundibugyo virus, a little known type, previously had caused just two small outbreaks. Now it’s at the center of a rapidly widening epidemic in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health workers disinfected a coffin containing the body of a man presumed have died of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Hegseth elimina de una lista de ascensos de la Marina a oficiales negros y mujeres
item.description: Las acciones de Hegseth, que parecen violar las normas del sistema de ascensos, fueron descritas por cuatro funcionarios actuales y antiguos del departamento que hablaron bajo condición de anonimato.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Kate Kelly
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La eliminación por parte del secretario de Defensa, Pete Hegseth, de al menos siete oficiales de la lista parece violar las normas que rigen el sistema de ascensos, según funcionarios actuales y antiguos del departamento.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘recondite’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en cinco artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: North Carolina Officer Charged After Beating of Suspect Captured on Doorbell Video
item.description: While trying to make an arrest, the officer, in Shelby, N.C., punched her repeatedly in the head and body and he continued even after another officer tried to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz

item.title: What to Know About Serena Williams’s Return to Tennis
item.description: On Monday, Serena Williams, 44, announced she would return to tennis. Ava Wallace, a tennis reporter for The Athletic, dives into what it means for a sport Williams once dominated.

item.title: Mavericks Pass on Dallas City Hall Site, Choose Valley View for New Arena
item.description: The choice gives the N.B.A. franchise room to pursue a sprawling mixed-use development while easing pressure on one of Dallas’s most closely watched civic battles.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dallas’s City Hall, designed by the architect I.M. Pei, opened in 1978.

item.title: U.S. Says More People May Be Charged in Terror Case Linked to Iran
item.description: A militia leader pleaded not guilty on Monday to what prosecutors say is his role in attacks that are part of Iran’s response to the war with Israel and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bensimon and Benjamin Weiser

item.title: Rick Adelman, Hall of Fame N.B.A. Coach, Dies at 79
item.description: His teams made the playoffs 16 times in 23 seasons, and he twice steered the Portland Trail Blazers to the league finals.
item.dc:creator: Harvey Araton

media:content.media:credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Rick Adelman during the 1990 N.B.A. playoffs. His teams won at least 50 games 11 times.

item.title: Crested Ibises Get the Royal Treatment in Japan as They Fly Again
item.description: Once widespread in Japan, the colorful birds went from being fairly commonplace in the country to being on the verge of extinction.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Kyodo News
media:content.media:description: Two crested ibises were released into the wild during a ceremony attended by Japan’s crown prince and princess on Sunday.

item.title: California Prepares for Governor Primary Race
item.description: On Tuesday, Californians will head to the polls to vote in the state’s primary election for governor and other offices. There are dozens of candidates for governor, but one Republican and a couple of Democrats have risen to the top of the polls.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Trump Stands to Gain a Key Ally in Colombia’s Upcoming Election
item.description: Abelardo De La Espriella, a hard-line candidate on the right facing a left-wing rival in a presidential runoff, has pledged to crush drug traffickers.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal and Luis Ferré-Sadurní
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Abelardo De La Espriella, at a campaign event in Medellín in May, captured more than 40 percent of the vote on Sunday.

item.title: Right-Wing Officials’ Appearance at Israel Day Parade Leads to Backlash
item.description: Parade organizers and New York lawmakers who marched in the parade sought to distance themselves from Bezalel Smotrich, a hard-line Israeli official who attended.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Eduardo Munoz/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Politicians rushed to release statements on Monday condemning Bezalel Smotrich and the other far-right Israeli officials who joined the Israel Day Parade, which took place Sunday on Fifth Avenue.

item.title: Map: 6.2-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes off the Coast of Italy
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Trump Finds High-Wire Iran Negotiations ‘Very Boring’
item.description: President Trump told CNBC that he “couldn’t care less” if the negotiations with Iran break down.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House in Washington in May.

item.title: Mette Frederiksen Forms New Government in Denmark
item.description: Mette Frederiksen may not be nearly as popular as she once was, but she remains the Danes’ most dominant leader in decades.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Amelia Nierenberg and Maya Tekeli

media:content.media:credit: Sebastian Elias Uth/Ritzau Scanpix Denmark, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen of Denmark speaking to reporters in front of the Royal Yacht Dannebrog on Monday.

item.title: Trump Administration to Dismantle Ocean Monitoring System
item.description: The $368 million network of instruments collecting data in both the Atlantic and Pacific has been critical to climate and ocean research.
item.dc:creator: Eric Niiler

media:content.media:credit: Rebecca Travis/WHOI
media:content.media:description: A mooring that was used in the Ocean Observatories Initiative was recovered after operating for a year in the Gulf of Alaska.

item.title: 5 People Leave Hantavirus Quarantine in Omaha
item.description: Thirteen people remain at the facility in Nebraska. Those who returned to their home states may be under constant surveillance for three more weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin

media:content.media:credit: Rebecca S. Gratz/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The Americans exposed to hantavirus have been isolated at the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.

item.title: Previewing California’s Primary Elections and High-Stakes Governor’s Race
item.description: The deep-blue state’s primary elections will show how angry voters are at the status quo.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Says Israel and Hezbollah Will Hold Off on New Military Action
item.description: A statement from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel did not mention a new cease-fire, but the Lebanese government said one was taking shape.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Christina Goldbaum, Johnatan Reiss and Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran on Saturday. Despite a cease-fire announced between Iran and the United States in early April, both countries have continued to trade strikes.

item.title: Minnesota Republicans Hold Moment of Silence for Derek Chauvin
item.description: Delegates to the Minnesota Republican Party’s convention voted to hold a moment of silence for Derek Chauvin, who was convicted of murdering George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Julie Bosman

media:content.media:credit: Richard Tsong-Taatarii/The Minnesota Star Tribune, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Delegates at the Minnesota Republican Party’s convention in Duluth, Minn., on Saturday.

item.title: Trump Backs Off Plans for $1.8 Billion Fund After Backlash
item.description: Also, Serena Williams is making a comeback. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times

item.title: Andy Halliday, a Star of ‘Vampire Lesbians of Sodom,’ Dies at 73
item.description: A beloved member of Charles Busch’s Theater in Limbo repertory, he had an irrepressibly comic stage presence that masked a shy, tender disposition.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: Trump Backs Off Plan for $1.8 Billion Fund That Drew Political Backlash
item.description: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Maggie Haberman, Alan Feuer, Devlin Barrett and Annie Karni

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Beyond the legal challenges, President Trump has also faced increasing pressure from both parties on Capitol Hill to torpedo the fund.

item.title: Demonstrators Cannot Be Forced to Take Down ‘86-47’ Flag, Judge Rules
item.description: A federal judge wrote that the phrase, which also led to charges against the former F.B.I. director, James Comey, did not appear to constitute a true threat.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Samuel Corum/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A protester waving a flag reading “8647” in front of the Supreme Court in Washington, in November.

item.title: Pentagon Bars Reporters From Its Press Office
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has repeatedly curtailed journalists’ access in the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Earlier in the year, the Defense Department moved to require that journalists in the Pentagon be accompanied by official escorts.
